commit.message:
Add 75 unit tests for financial VibeLiquidStaking (stVIBE)

Comprehensive test coverage for the production liquid staking contract:
- Initialization (proxy deployment, zero-oracle revert, ETH-only mode)
- ETH staking (first deposit 1:1, share dilution after rewards, anti-MEV timestamp)
- VIBE staking (token transfer, OnlyVibeMode guard)
- Withdrawal queue (request, claim after 7-day unbonding, double-claim, wrong owner)
- Instant unstake (0.5% fee, 1-day hold period, insufficient shares)
- Oracle rewards (insurance cut, operator commissions, sanity cap, staleness)
- Node operators (add/remove/toggle/commission, swap-and-pop, 128 limit)
- Insurance pool (cover slashing, withdraw, insufficient funds)
- Admin (set oracle, set vibe token, withdraw fees)
- Views (share price, pooled ETH, TVL, withdrawal request, operator details)
- Integration (full lifecycle, monotonic share price, withdrawal neutrality)
- Edge cases (receive ETH, zero-validator operators, empty pool rewards)

All 75 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix circular-print crash and add REPL integration tests

Fix ece-write/ece-display to use format-ece-proc for compiled procedures
instead of prin1, which caused infinite recursion on circular environment
references (e.g. when defining a function at the REPL).

crash regression, error recovery, string/boolean/lambda printing, and EOF.

commit.message:
refactor(scheduler): interleave local and global lanes in ThreeLaneWorker dispatch

Restructure ThreeLaneWorker::next_task dispatch to interleave local
PriorityScheduler and global queue checks instead of processing all
global queues before local ones. The new two-phase approach:

Phase 1: Check the highest-priority global queue based on the
scheduling suggestion (timed for MeetDeadlines, cancel for default).

Phase 2: Acquire the local PriorityScheduler lock once and check
remaining cancel/timed lanes in strict suggestion order, interleaving
with global fallbacks.

This reduces latency for locally-queued high-priority tasks that were
previously blocked behind the full global queue scan.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(io): stop read_line on definite UTF-8 errors even without newline

Fix process_fresh_chunk and process_pending_chunk to immediately
finish with an invalid-data error when the UTF-8 validation reports
a definite error (error_len().is_some()), even if no newline has been
found yet. Previously, the code only finished on newline, causing
indefinite reads when the stream contained malformed bytes that would
never produce a valid newline.

Three cases fixed:
- Fresh chunk with Utf8Error that has a definite error length
- Pending chunk with Utf8Error that has a definite error length
- Pending chunk where error_len is definite but no valid_len prefix

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(service): load balancer re-polls backends that self-wake during probe

Fix LoadBalancer backend selection to handle services that become ready
on a follow-up poll after self-waking during the initial poll_ready
probe.

- Replace noop_waker in poll_service_ready_once with a tracked_probe_
  waker that records whether wake_by_ref was called during the probe.
- If a backend returns Pending but the waker was triggered (indicating
  the service became ready synchronously during the poll, e.g. after
  acquiring an internal semaphore), re-poll it once more before moving
  to the next candidate.
- This prevents skipping backends that are effectively ready but use a
  two-phase poll pattern (common with concurrency limiters and pools).

Includes WakeDuringPollReadyService test helper and associated tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Remove vestigial Freeze Encoder Layers spinner from UNetHandler

The spinner sent freeze_encoder_layers as a handler parameter but no
Python code ever read it. Layer freezing is handled by TrainingDialog's
LayerFreezePanel which sends frozen_layers (list of named layers) via
TrainingConfig. With the encoder combo already removed, UNetHandler no
longer needs a custom training UI at all.

fix(service): rate limiter now checks inner service readiness before granting token

Rewrite RateLimit::poll_ready to check inner service readiness after
acquiring a token, rolling back the token if the inner service is not
ready. Previously, poll_ready_with_time only checked token availability
without consulting the inner service, which could lead to dispatching
requests to a service that wasn't ready to handle them.

Also:
- Generalize poll_ready_with_time to accept a Request type parameter,
  matching the Service trait's generic signature.
- Deduplicate the poll_ready implementation in Service::poll_ready by
  delegating to poll_ready_with_time, eliminating ~40 lines of
  copy-pasted refill/sleep logic.
- Add reserved_tokens tracking for token rollback on inner-service
  Pending/Err.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(database): add SleepFn hook to DbPool for deterministic testing

Extend the database connection pool with a configurable sleep hook
alongside the existing TimeGetter:

- Add SleepFn type alias (fn(Duration)) for synchronous retry/backoff
  loops that need to sleep outside async context.
- Add with_time_getter_and_sleep_fn constructor that accepts both hooks.
- Existing with_time_getter preserved as convenience (defaults sleep to
  thread::sleep).
- Add sleep_fn() accessor for downstream consumers.
- Type alias TimeGetter replaces raw fn() -> Instant throughout for
  clearer documentation.

This enables fully deterministic pool testing where both time reads
and sleep calls can be intercepted and controlled.

commit.message:
feat: collect commits from all branches for accurate merge ratio

Previously only default branch commits were collected, making merge ratio
always 100%. Now uses git log --all to collect from all branches and
git rev-list to determine actual default branch ancestry. Includes
deduplication for commits reachable from multiple branches.

Known limitation: squash merges replace original feature branch commits,
so abandoned branches with squash-merged commits may not be counted.

commit.message:
fix: always fall back to local OCR when remote OCR returns 500

Previously, ocr_remote_required=True caused remote OCR failures to
re-raise the exception, blocking extraction entirely. Users had to
run OCR twice to process affected documents. Now all remote OCR
failures gracefully fall back to Google Vision / local OCR.

commit.message:
Overhaul humanizer engine based on Wikipedia AI writing signals

Major expansion of the humanization pipeline with comprehensive AI
signal detection and removal:

- Add 6 new pattern dictionaries: PUFFERY_SWAPS (significance/legacy
  phrases), PROMOTIONAL_SWAPS (ad-like language), DIDACTIC_SWAPS
  (unnecessary disclaimers), VAGUE_ATTRIBUTION_SWAPS (weasel wording),
  plus expanded TRANSITION_SWAPS and WORD_SWAPS
- Add trailing -ing phrase removal (e.g. ", highlighting the...")
- Add "not only X but also Y" simplification
- Add rule-of-three adjective reduction
- Add challenges/prospects formula rewriting
- Add smart/curly quote normalization
- Add em dash overuse reduction
- Expand AI vocabulary coverage with all verb forms (-s, -ed, -ing)
- Total pattern count: ~250+ (up from ~90)

- Brunnermeier & Pedersen (2009) - RFS 22(6), 2201-2238 ✓
- Liu, Tsyvinski & Wu (2022) - JF 77(2), 1133-1177 ✓

Fix AGE Cypher queries: use exec_driver_sql to avoid bind param parsing

SQLAlchemy text() interprets [:MENTIONS] and [:CO_OCCURS] as bind
parameters. Switch all Cypher queries to _cypher() helper using
exec_driver_sql which bypasses parameter parsing.

commit.message:
fix: proxy API via Next.js rewrites to fix cross-origin login

Replace NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL (build-time, unreliable) with Next.js
rewrites that proxy /api/* to BACKEND_URL at runtime. The browser
now calls relative /api/* on the same origin - no CORS, no build-time
embedding issues, no SameSite cookie problems.

swapPrev was disabling pointer-events on the back card (arriving),

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add thin-film interference interactive section to SS304 Colour Physics

New section "Interference in Action" between Oxide Growth and Temperature:
- Canvas cross-section showing oxide layer (height scales with slider),
  incident white light, Ray 1 (reflects at surface) and Ray 2 (travels
  through film, reflects at steel, exits) with phase-flip labels and
  OPD = 2nd bracket annotation
- Reflected spectrum strip computed per-wavelength via Airy two-beam
  formula (r1=0.40, r2=0.62, n=2.3)
- Reference white-light strip for comparison
- Colour swatch computed from CIE 1931 XYZ → sRGB (physically derived,
  not a lookup table)
- Readout shows OPD, cancelled wavelength and reinforced wavelength for
  current thickness

Physics: filmR(λ,d) = Airy formula with Cr₂O₃ refractive index 2.3;
specToRGB() integrates CIE 1931 standard observer over the reflected
spectrum for accurate display colours.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add all 12 features: caching, latest detection, error types, streaming, writer, rendering, WASM, models, search, multi-fhour, DataFrame, PyPI

Tier 1 — Ship it:
- PyPI release prep: metadata, classifiers, musllinux wheels, __version__
- Latest run detection: fetch("hrrr", "latest") probes AWS for most recent run
- Disk caching: platform-specific cache dir, FNV-1a hashing, fail-safe ops
- Proper error types: RustmetError enum with thiserror, replaces String errors

Tier 2 — Feature parity+:
- Multi-forecast-hour: fhour=[0,1,2,3] or "0-12:3", parallel downloads with rayon
- Inventory as DataFrame: inventory_df(), to_dataframe(), pandas optional dep
- Fuzzy search: search("temperature 2m") with aliases and relevance scoring
- 5 new models: ECMWF IFS, NBM, RRFS, RTMA, HREF (9 total)

Tier 3 — Beyond:
- Streaming decode: StreamingParser, fetch_iter() generator, incremental parsing
- GRIB2 writer: round-trip verified simple packing, Lambert/lat-lon grids, bitmaps
- Built-in rendering: 7 colormaps, marching squares contours, rayon rasterization, PNG
- WASM build: feature-gated network/JPEG2000, wasm-bindgen, browser-ready

103 tests passing, 0 failures. Clean debug + release builds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Enforce 1-day hold period in mechanism VibeLiquidStaking

The stakedAt mapping was tracked but never checked on unstake.

sandwich attacks on reward distribution.

commit.message:
Floquet multiplier interpretation FALSIFIED — revert to VEV, add evidence

Numerical verification (floquet_numerical_verify.py) shows the Floquet
multiplier at E=0 scales as ~exp(4K²), NOT as 1/q = phi. Dead claim #20.

Reverts significant.md, significant-companion.md, claude assess.md to
VEV interpretation (phi = Phi_+ from Dvali-Shifman 1997). Gap 1 remains
at grade B-. Updates START-HERE.md and gap1_floquet_closure.py headers.

What holds: theta_3/theta_4 = det_AP/det_P (proven), golden cascade
(modes 1+2 = phi^8, algebraic), Hill discriminant sqrt(5).
